reasoning: The latest inflation data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics for September 2025 shows a Year-over-Year CPI of 3.0%. Inflation has been steadily rising since its low of 2.3% in April 2025. Major forecasters predict inflation will remain elevated through the end of 2025, with the Federal Reserve's FOMC projecting 3.1% and some investment banks anticipating even higher rates due to the inflationary impact of new tariffs implemented by the Trump administration. Given the established upward trend and the consensus among economists that tariff and immigration policies will continue to put upward pressure on prices, a sudden drop to below 2.5% by the end of 2025 or an annual average below 2.5% is highly improbable.
